records.ety: [L. compurgatio, fr. compurgare to purify wholly; com- + purgare to make pure. See Purge, v. t.]

defs: The act or practice of justifying or confirming a man's veracity by the oath of others; -- called also wager of law. See Purgation; also Wager of law, under Wager.
Exculpation by testimony to one's veracity or innocence.
quotes: He was privileged from his childhood from suspicion of incontinency and needed no compurgation.
